title: The Father
logline: An aging man with dementia struggles to understand his changing reality as his daughter tries to care for him while his perception of people, places, and time becomes increasingly unreliable.
antagonistOrConflict: Dementia and the deterioration of Anthony's grip on reality

actBreaks.description: Anthony's reality fundamentally shifts as his flat transforms into Anne's flat, marking the beginning of his deeper confusion about his living situation.

validation.ruling: The camera contemplates the empty living room that has transformed from Anthony's flat into Anne and Paul's modern space, marking the irreversible shift from Anthony's perspective to the objective reality of his situation.

actBreaks.description: Anthony finds himself in a hospital corridor during the night, representing the complete breakdown of his ability to distinguish between reality and confusion.

validation.ruling: Anthony finds himself in an undeniable hospital corridor at night and sees Lucy in a hospital bed, representing the final collapse of his constructed reality and transition into the hospital setting where the climax will occur.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a brilliant subjective reality structure that places the audience directly inside the experience of dementia. Rather than following traditional three-act progression, the story unfolds through Anthony's increasingly unreliable perception, with the same physical spaces transforming to reflect his mental state. The flat itself becomes a character, morphing from Anthony's home to Anne's flat to a care facility, mirroring his loss of autonomy. The structure is deliberately disorienting, using repetition and variation to create the confusion Anthony experiences - the same conversations happen with different people, familiar faces become strangers, and time becomes fluid.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates the disorientation as the script progresses. Early scenes (1-22) establish a relatively stable reality with longer dialogue exchanges. The middle section (23-73) introduces rapid shifts between different versions of the same spaces, with shorter, more fragmented scenes. The final act (74-88) slows down again but with devastating emotional weight, culminating in the quiet revelation of Anthony's true situation in the hospital.

incitingIncident.description: Anthony discovers a stranger named Paul reading emails in his living room; Paul claims to live there while Anthony insists it's his flat, leading to Paul calling Anne.

validation.ruling: Anthony discovers a stranger named Paul in his living room who claims to live there and be Anne's husband of ten years, completely shattering Anthony's understanding of his world and establishing the central conflict of his deteriorating grasp on reality.

midpoint.description: Anthony emerges from his bedroom and slows down to eavesdrop as Paul tells Anne they need to find another arrangement and put Anthony in a nursing home.

validation.ruling: The candidate dinner scene lacks the transformative power of a midpoint. Scene 57 is the true midpoint where Anthony overhears Paul telling Anne they need to put him in a nursing home, marking his shift from passive confusion to active awareness of the threat to his independence.

climax.description: Anthony goes back into his room where the Woman in a white coat brings his medication, and he realizes she's a nurse who's been looking after him for weeks, leading to his complete breakdown as he cries for his mother.

validation.ruling: Anthony confronts the ultimate reality of his condition in the hospital room, desperately asking about Anne and clinging to memories of his daughters while facing the nurse who represents his complete loss of independence and connection to his former life.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN LONDON DAY
scenes.summary: Anne emerges from a London tube station in a hurry with a serious expression, rushes across the street nearly getting hit by a taxi, and enters a block of flats.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRS AND LANDING DAY
scenes.summary: Anne climbs stairs to the second floor, rings a bell, then impatiently uses her own keys to open the door and calls for her dad.

scenes.heading: INT. ANTHONY’S FLAT DAY
scenes.summary: Anne moves anxiously from room to room calling for her dad with mounting tension as she receives no answer.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE DAY
scenes.summary: Anne finds Anthony in his office listening to music on headphones; he's surprised and irritated by her unexpected arrival and asks what she's doing there.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Anne confronts Anthony about calling his caregiver a bitch and threatening her physically, while Anthony denies remembering and claims the woman is crazy.

scenes.heading: INT. ANTHONY’S BATHROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony goes to his bathroom, closes the door to check his hiding place, and emerges with his watch on his wrist with satisfaction.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Anne waits in the living room, notices her sister's painting on the wall, and sees Anthony go into his office and turn on the TV as if he's forgotten she's there.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE DAY
scenes.summary: Anne finds Anthony watching Fred Astaire on TV; they discuss his found watch and he insists he hid it from Angela, takes his pills, and compares Anne unfavorably to her sister.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BEDROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony watches from his bedroom window as Anne crosses the street and walks away without looking back, then sits on his bed feeling anxious about his daughter threatening him.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony makes tea while humming to Verdi, finds mysterious shopping bags on the table, then hears a door closing and footsteps in the flat.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRANCE HALLWAY DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony cautiously approaches the sounds, calls out for Anne, and hears another sound from the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony discovers a stranger named Paul reading emails in his living room; Paul claims to live there while Anthony insists it's his flat, leading to Paul calling Anne.
scenes.turningPointNote: First major reality shift - Anthony encounters a stranger who claims to live in his flat, fundamentally challenging his grip on reality

scenes.heading: INT. INDIAN SHOP DAY
scenes.summary: Anne is shopping for chicken in an Indian supermarket when she receives Paul's phone call asking what's the matter.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Paul tells Anne that Anthony isn't feeling well and would like to see her, while Anthony discreetly pockets a fork and tells Paul that Anne is moving to Paris with a Frenchman.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN DAY
scenes.summary: Paul opens wine while Anthony explains about a nurse that Anne wants to hire, insisting he can manage on his own even if Anne goes abroad.

scenes.heading: INT. ENTRANCE HALLWAY DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony continues talking to Paul in the entrance hallway about Anne needing to go abroad and his ability to manage independently.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony tries to remember Paul's name and argues he can manage alone, criticizing Anne's intelligence while Paul defends her efforts to help him.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony encounters a completely different woman taking off her coat who calls him 'dad,' but he doesn't recognize her and recoils when she approaches.
scenes.turningPointNote: Second major reality shift - the woman Anthony thought was Anne is now a complete stranger, deepening his disorientation

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM DAY
scenes.summary: The woman approaches Anthony with concern as he explains finding Paul in his kitchen, but she reveals she's not married and divorced five years ago, confusing Anthony about Paul's identity.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony searches the empty kitchen for Paul, insisting he was just there.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony finds the dining room empty while the woman suggests he's mistaken; she smiles when he mentions 'the man with the chicken,' driving Anthony to frustration.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BEDROOM 2 DAY - PT1
scenes.summary: Anthony enters a bedroom that occupies the same space as his but with different décor, notices the changes, looks out at the familiar London street view, and tells the woman something funny is going on.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: The camera contemplates the empty living room, which now has the characteristics of Anne and Paul's flat rather than Anthony's place.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY AND KITCHEN 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne returns with shopping while on the phone discussing her father's condition, mentioning he didn't recognize her the other day, and places bags in the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne hangs up her coat, picks up letters, and heads to the office while still on the phone.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne works at her desk in what appears to be her translator's office, which occupies the same space as Anthony's office but with different décor.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony lies in bed in pajamas working on a crossword, then stops and stares anxiously into space, wondering why he feels he's no longer in his own flat.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne closes her computer when the bell rings and heads apprehensively to answer the front door.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne opens the door to Laura, a caregiver, and welcomes her inside while thanking her for coming.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM 2 DAY - PT1
scenes.summary: Anne explains to Laura that her father is upset about needing care and warns that he may react unexpectedly, while Laura asks about his living situation.

scenes.heading: INT. ANTHONY’S BATHROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony finds his watch in the bathroom, checks his appearance in the mirror, and heads back to the living room satisfied.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony charms Laura by offering aperitifs, boasting about having two watches, and sharing drinks while making disparaging comments about Anne and praising his other daughter Lucy.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN 2 NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anne drinks herbal tea alone in the kitchen late at night, drops and breaks a cup, then briefly breaks down crying before pulling herself together.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR 2 NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anne turns off lights and checks on her sleeping father like a child, then sits on his bed edge.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM 2 NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anne sits emotionally by Anthony's bedside, puts her hand on his cheek, then moves it to his throat and begins strangling her sleeping father.
scenes.turningPointNote: Shocking moment of violence as Anne appears to strangle Anthony - though this may be fantasy/nightmare given the film's unreliable reality

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne snaps out of a daydream while setting the table as Paul asks about Laura's visit, and Anne confirms Laura will start tomorrow.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne burns herself on the oven and appears jumpy; Paul comforts her as she explains Anthony didn't recognize her earlier, making her feel like a stranger to him.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony watches Paul suspiciously from a distance, becomes fascinated by Paul's watch wondering if it might be his own, while Anne mentions Laura's visit.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony complains to Anne about his missing watch, insisting it must be somewhere or stolen, while Anne offers to look for it in his cupboard.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony asks Paul for the time and admires his watch, requesting to see it while Paul mentions the chicken Anne bought and Laura's successful visit.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BATHROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne finds Anthony's watch and a fork hidden in his bathroom cupboard.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. KITCHEN 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne checks the chicken in the oven, opens the kitchen window to smoke a cigarette, and watches a laughing couple in an apartment across the street.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony tells Paul about his relationship with his daughters, praising Lucy as marvelous while criticizing Anne, as Paul impatiently moves to get more wine.

scenes.heading: INT. LIFT DAY
scenes.summary: Anne has a new hairdo as she and Anthony ride in a lift; Anthony notices and compliments her appearance before the doors open.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRS AND LANDING DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony and Anne exit the lift onto what looks like his flat's landing, but Anne rings the bell and they enter what turns out to be a medical surgery.

scenes.heading: INT. SURGERY DAY
scenes.summary: Anne leads the disoriented Anthony through a surgery waiting area with startling colored chairs that resembles his flat's entrance hall but is furnished differently.

scenes.heading: INT. DOCTOR’S OFFICE DAY
scenes.summary: A doctor questions Anthony about his birth date and living situation; Anthony insists Anne is moving to Paris while Anne denies this, leading to his confusion about her changing plans.

scenes.heading: INT. WAITING ROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony sits worried in the waiting room watching Anne and the doctor talk privately in the distance, becoming suspicious when Anne smiles at him after receiving the doctor's card.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI DAY
scenes.summary: In a taxi, Anthony pulls his hand away when Anne tries to reach for it and turns away from her to stare out the window.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne irons clothes in the kitchen, then takes the laundry basket to Anthony's bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. ANTHONY'S BEDROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne puts away Anthony's shirt and touches his dark suits and polished shoes, then sits on his bed looking at a family photo while contemplating what to do with her father.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. INDIAN SHOP DAY
scenes.summary: Anne walks to an Indian shop to buy chicken when her phone rings and she agrees to come up in a minute after hearing something is wrong.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne returns with bags and Paul tells her that Anthony would like to see her and is in his room.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR 2 / BEDROOM 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne watches Anthony struggling helplessly to put on his sweater before crossing to help him, and he looks at her with benevolence having forgotten their recent conflict.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN 2 EVENING
scenes.summary: Anne prepares chicken for dinner in the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM 2 EVENING
scenes.summary: Anthony reads a book, checks his watch, closes the book and gets up for dinner.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR 2 EVENING
scenes.summary: Anthony emerges from his bedroom and slows down to eavesdrop as Paul tells Anne they need to find another arrangement and put Anthony in a nursing home.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM 2 EVENING
scenes.summary: Anthony appears unnoticed in the dining room doorway as Paul tells Anne that Anthony is ill and the moment will come when even a good carer won't be enough.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM 2 EVENING
scenes.summary: During an awkward dinner, Paul confronts Anthony about having a daughter who sacrifices everything for him while he shows no gratitude, leading to Anthony leaving the table.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul's direct confrontation with Anthony about his lack of gratitude escalates the family conflict to a breaking point

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN 2 EVENING
scenes.summary: Anthony goes to the kitchen but can't remember why he came and stands there frowning with his plate.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM 2 EVENING
scenes.summary: Anne confronts Paul about speaking harshly in front of Anthony, and Paul argues that Anthony has completely lost it and they need to put him in an institution.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM 2 NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anne sits on the edge of Anthony's bed after he's gone to sleep, strokes his cheek tenderly, then gets up and leaves like a mother putting her child to bed.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE 2 DAY
scenes.summary: Anne sits at her office desk holding the doctor's card, hesitates for a long time, then dials a number and hears waiting room muzak while staring at light reflections from a crystal pyramid.

scenes.heading: INT. FLAT 3 MORNING
scenes.summary: The flat appears with less furniture and packing cases, with Lucy's painting no longer on the wall, as the doctor's voice comes over the phone line.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM 3 MORNING
scenes.summary: Anthony opens his bedroom curtains and looks out at the London street, watching a child play with a plastic bag before his smile brutally disappears.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR 3 MORNING
scenes.summary: Anthony moves down the corridor in pyjamas and notices brightly-colored chairs from the surgery now in the hallway while Anne calls to him from elsewhere.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM 3 MORNING - PT 1 & PT 2 (ANNE ONLY)
scenes.summary: Anthony discovers packing cases and notices Lucy's painting is gone from the wall, asks Anne about it but she says there was never a painting there, then Laura emerges from the kitchen with coffee while Anne is absent.
scenes.turningPointNote: Anthony's reality becomes increasingly unstable as familiar objects disappear and people appear unexpectedly

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN 3 EVENING
scenes.summary: Anne smokes at the kitchen window when she hears Anthony's voice calling her and hurries to the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM 3 EVENING
scenes.summary: Anthony is in a defensive hunched position while Paul looks at him uncomprehendingly, and Anne comforts Anthony about his watch while holding him in her arms.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM 3 EVENING
scenes.summary: Anne brushes her teeth in the bathroom mirror, stops and looks exhausted and unhappy, then steps back into the bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN BEDROOM 3 EVENING
scenes.summary: Paul is already in bed when Anne comes to sit on the bed.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM 3 NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anthony wakes in the middle of the night hearing voices calling him Dad, gets up thinking it might be Lucy, and leaves his room.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR 3 NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anthony hears a sound from a cupboard in the dark corridor, opens it, and discovers it leads to a hospital corridor he's seeing for the first time.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anthony finds himself bewildered in a hospital corridor at night and sees Lucy stretched out on a bed through a half-open door, who turns and calls him Dad.
scenes.turningPointNote: Anthony's reality completely shifts as he encounters his deceased daughter Lucy in what appears to be a hospital

scenes.heading: INT. FLAT 4 MORNING
scenes.summary: The flat appears almost completely empty with hardly any furniture at all.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM 4 MORNING
scenes.summary: Anthony splashes water on his face, looks in the mirror, tidies his hair, and remembers his dream about the cupboard.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR 4 MORNING
scenes.summary: Anthony approaches the cupboard from his dream reluctantly, opens it to find just a broom cupboard, then hears Anne calling him.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN 4 MORNING
scenes.summary: Anne serves Anthony breakfast and reminds him about his visitor Laura, while Anthony says he dreamt about her and that she reminds him of Lucy, but then asks who Anne is talking about when she mentions his new carer.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN 4 DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony hides behind the breakfast table in his pyjamas as Anne and Laura arrive, but to his amazement it's not Laura but the Woman instead.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM 5 DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony sits on a bed in a clearly different bedroom staring into space when Anne appears wearing a coat, sits beside him, and struggles to tell him something about when he first came to their place.
scenes.turningPointNote: Anne begins the conversation about moving Anthony out of their home, marking a major transition in his living situation

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL DAY
scenes.summary: The setting is now clearly revealed to be a hospital.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL PARK DAY
scenes.summary: Anne comes out of the hospital building and walks away, leaving it behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET OUTSIDE HOSPITAL DAY
scenes.summary: Anne hails a taxi outside the hospital gates and takes a last look back at the hospital before getting in.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI DAY
scenes.summary: Anne settles into the taxi as it leaves and fights back tears.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony wakes from a siesta in a hospital room, sees medications on his bedside table, discovers his watch is missing again, and calls for Anne.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR DAY
scenes.summary: Anthony pushes open his door and finds himself facing the hospital corridor from his nightmare.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. HOSPITAL ROOM DAY - PT 1
scenes.summary: Anthony goes back into his room where the Woman in a white coat brings his medication, and he realizes she's a nurse who's been looking after him for weeks, leading to his complete breakdown as he cries for his mother.
scenes.turningPointNote: Anthony's final devastating realization of his situation culminates in his complete emotional collapse

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL PARK DAY
scenes.summary: The wind blows through trees in the hospital park, rustling the leaves.
